title: In-pi agent confabulates substrate vocabulary/signatures instead of grounding in the discovery tools — no anti-confabulation directive in the agent-reachable layer
description: [FOLDED INTO FGAP-085 per user direction 2026-05-22 — grounding-directive content + write-time fail-loud facet now live in FGAP-085.] Live in-pi agents (2026-05-22) repeatedly ASSERTED substrate specifics from assumption rather than DERIVING them from the queryable surface: (a) used relation_type `phase_depends_on` which is NOT registered in config.relation_types (verified empty) — a confabulated edge type; (b) proposed reconstructing draft-07 schemas from read-samples-catalog SHAPE metadata (it returns arrayKeys/itemProperties, not bodies) instead of knowing install is the materialization path; (c) assumed builtin filesystem read of the npm package (a constrained --tools session has no read builtin); (d) guessed tool signatures (append-block-item arrayKey param, an invented roadmap.json shape) instead of consulting list-tools. The unifying ignorance: the agent does not know that the VOCABULARY (relation_types/block_kinds/lenses via read-config + read-samples-catalog) and its OWN tool signatures (via list-tools) are queryable, so it should ground before asserting and never invent canonical names or schema bodies. The surface compounds it: append-relation DEFERS relation_type-registration validation (FGAP-075), so a confabulated edge writes silently and only fails at project-validate — no write-time correction. This is the CONTENT gap (what the agent must be told), distinct from FGAP-085 (description quality = the channel) and FGAP-090 (skills don't reach the agent = the channel reach). Because skills are unreachable, the cure must live in the always-present layer (tool descriptions + a foundational orientation).

evidence.reference: relation_types has no `phase_depends_on` (verified empty for phase*) — the agent invented it
read-samples-catalog returns shape (arrayKeys/itemProperties), NOT raw schema bodies — yet the agent proposed reconstructing schemas from it

read-config / read-samples-catalog / list-tools expose the vocabulary + signatures the agent should ground in but didn't; append-relation defers relation_type validation (no write-time confabulation check)

impact: A harness-confined agent silently produces invalid substrate (unregistered-relation_type edges, mis-shaped items, reconstructed-wrong schemas) because nothing in its reachable context tells it to ground in the discovery tools or forbids inventing canonical names. Deferred validation means the error surfaces late (project-validate) or never (if validate isn't run). This undermines the DEC-0014 aim: a tools-only agent that operates the substrate CORRECTLY, not just plausibly.
proposed_resolution: Encode an explicit grounding directive in the always-present layer (tool descriptions per FGAP-085, and/or a foundational in-pi orientation surfaced regardless of skills per FGAP-090): (1) vocabulary is queryable — call read-config / read-samples-catalog before referencing any relation_type/block_kind/lens; never invent canonical names; (2) tool signatures are queryable via list-tools — consult, don't guess; (3) bootstrap/materialization is init -> accept-all -> install (FGAP-091), not schema reconstruction. Consider tightening append-relation/amend-config to fail loud on unregistered names at write (reversing the FGAP-075/076 deferral where layering permits) so confabulation can't write silently. Sequence with FGAP-085 (descriptions) + FGAP-090 (skill-reach decision).
